skill.name: create-skill
skill.description: Guides agents through creating valid Agent Skills with required metadata, concise instructions, and optional supporting resources.
skill.summary: Create well-structured Agent Skills with valid metadata and documentation.
skill.author: 0xmsc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and weak-crypto patterns, but review found these are Markdown code fences, inline file names, YAML examples, and documentation text rather than executable code. The only remaining minor concern is two external documentation links in references/what-are-skills.md; no evidence of malicious intent, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or runnable network code was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Syntax Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the reviewed locations are Markdown code fences and inline identifiers such as SKILL.md, scripts, references, and mkdir examples. These are documentation examples and are not executable code paths in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown examples or inline documentation tokens, not Ruby code or shell execution. No executable script file or command runner is present in the scanned file set.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links Present
low_findings.description: The skill includes two hardcoded URLs to public documentation and example repositories. This is a minor marketplace review concern because users may follow external links, but there is no programmatic network request or exfiltration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plain Markdown links in a reference document. They do not execute automatically and do not include tokens, tracking parameters, or suspicious destinations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Patterns Are Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts map to frontmatter descriptions, Markdown file names, YAML examples, and PDF-related sample text. No cryptographic function, hash routine, or security-sensitive implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain prose and sample skill metadata. There is no code that performs MD5, SHA1, encryption, signing, or password handling.

content.value_statement: Writing a useful Agent Skill requires clear metadata, concise instructions, and well-organized references. This skill gives agents a repeatable process for producing valid skill folders.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required Agent Skill folder structure and SKILL.md file.
Defines required frontmatter fields for skill name and description.
Guides when to add scripts, references, and assets directories.
Encourages concise instruction writing and progressive disclosure.
Points agents to detailed specification and design reference files.
limitations: Does not include automated validation scripts or schema tooling.
Does not generate complete skills without user-provided requirements.
Does not enforce marketplace policy or security review by itself.
Reference links may require internet access if users choose to open them.

use_cases.title: Create a New Skill Folder
use_cases.description: Use the skill to plan the folder, SKILL.md metadata, and optional resource directories for a new agent workflow.

use_cases.title: Improve Skill Instructions
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to keep instructions concise, imperative, and organized for agent loading.

use_cases.title: Review Skill Structure
use_cases.description: Compare an existing skill against the required frontmatter, naming rules, and reference organization guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner author creating a simple text-only skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-skill guidance to draft a simple Agent Skill for this workflow: [describe workflow]. Include the name, description, and main instruction sections.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Skill Resources
prompt_templates.scenario: Author deciding whether references, scripts, or assets are needed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design the folder structure for an Agent Skill about [topic]. Decide which optional directories are justified and explain what each one should contain.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Existing Skill Format
prompt_templates.scenario: Maintainer checking a skill before sharing it.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Agent Skill structure against the create-skill requirements. Check the frontmatter, directory names, instruction length, and reference organization.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Advanced Skill Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced author optimizing a complex skill for progressive disclosure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-skill specification to restructure this complex skill for progressive disclosure. Move detailed material into references and keep SKILL.md focused on activation steps.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for reviewing pull requests.
output: A proposed skill name that follows lowercase hyphen naming rules.
A clear description that states what the skill does and when to use it.
A concise instruction outline with optional references for detailed review criteria.

output_examples.input: Check whether my skill layout is valid.
output: A short validation summary for the folder structure and frontmatter.
Specific notes about missing or unnecessary scripts, references, or assets directories.
Actionable edits to make the skill easier for agents to load and follow.

output_examples.input: Turn a long workflow document into a skill.
output: A compact main instruction file focused on when and how to use the workflow.
Suggested reference files for detailed procedures and examples.
A checklist for keeping the final skill maintainable.

best_practices: Write descriptions that include both the task and the trigger context.
Keep the main SKILL.md concise and move detailed material into references.
Create optional directories only when they contain real supporting content.
anti_patterns: Using vague descriptions that do not tell agents when to activate the skill.
Putting long reference material directly in the main instruction file.
Creating empty scripts, references, or assets folders without a clear purpose.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create Agent Skills with valid metadata, concise instructions, and optional supporting resources.

faq.question: Does it include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is documentation only and does not include runnable script files.

faq.question: Can it validate a skill automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and checks, but it does not include an automated validator.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why were static security alerts reported?
faq.answer: Markdown backticks, file names, and examples matched broad scanner patterns for commands and weak cryptography.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low risk and no evidence of malicious behavior or executable dangerous patterns.
